slides.title: Story Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn the key words from the story of the two goats:

🐐 goat — an animal with horns that lives on mountains
🏔️ mountain — a very high, rocky place
😤 stubborn — not wanting to change your mind
😡 angry — feeling mad, not happy at all
💧 wet — covered in water
slides.tip: Stubborn = terco/a. A stubborn person never wants to move or change!

slides.title: Two Stubborn Little Goats
slides.html:
Two little goats lived on a mountain. One day, they met on a narrow bridge over a river.

“Move! I want to cross!” said the first goat. She was very stubborn.

“No, YOU move!” said the second goat. He was stubborn too!

They both got angry. They pushed and pushed. Then... SPLASH! They both fell into the river and got very wet!

The lesson? Don't be too stubborn. It's better to share!

slides.title: Superlative Adjectives
slides.content:
Superlative adjectives compare one thing to ALL the others in a group:

For short adjectives, add the + -est:

📏 tall → the tallest
🚀 fast → the fastest
💪 strong → the strongest

Example: The giraffe is the tallest animal.
slides.tip: Superlative = THE BEST of all! Use THE before the superlative.

slides.title: Irregular and Long Superlatives
slides.content:
Some superlatives are irregular — they don't follow the rules:

👍 good → the best (NOT the goodest!)

For long adjectives (2+ syllables), use the most:

🌟 popular → the most popular
🎢 exciting → the most exciting

Example: Football is the most popular sport in Spain.
slides.tip: Short adjectives: the + adjective + -est. Long adjectives: the most + adjective.

slides.title: Write the Superlative
slides.problem:
Write the superlative form of each adjective:

1. tall → _____
2. fast → _____
3. good → _____
4. popular → _____
5. exciting → _____
steps: 1. tall is short (1 syllable) --> the tallest
2. fast is short (1 syllable) --> the fastest
4. popular is long (3 syllables) --> the most popular
5. exciting is long (3 syllables) --> the most exciting
slides.answer: 1. the tallest  2. the fastest  3. the best  4. the most popular  5. the most exciting

slides.title: Comparative vs Superlative
slides.content:
What's the difference?

📏 Comparative = compares TWO things:
A goat is faster than a turtle.

🏆 Superlative = compares ONE to ALL the rest:
The cheetah is the fastest animal.

Comparative: adjective + -er + than
Superlative: the + adjective + -est

slides.title: Comparative or Superlative?
slides.problem:
Choose the correct form:

1. An elephant is _____ (big) than a dog.
2. The blue whale is _____ (big) animal in the world.
3. This book is _____ (exciting) than that one.
4. This is _____ (good) day of my life!
steps: 2. Comparing to ALL animals --> superlative: the biggest
3. Comparing TWO books (long adjective) --> comparative: more exciting
4. Comparing to ALL days (irregular) --> superlative: the best
slides.answer: 1. bigger  2. the biggest  3. more exciting  4. the best

slides.title: The Mountain Race
slides.html:
Three goats lived on a mountain: Billy, Nanny, and Kid.

Billy was the strongest goat. Nanny was the fastest. And Kid was the smallest but also the most popular because he was kind to everyone.

One day they had a race. Nanny was faster than Billy. Billy was stronger than Kid. But Kid had the best idea: “Let's all cross the bridge together!”

Nobody got angry. Nobody got wet. It was the most exciting day on the mountain!

slides.title: Spelling Rules for Superlatives
slides.content:
Watch out for these spelling rules:

🔹 Adjectives ending in -e: just add -st
large → the largest

🔹 Adjectives ending in consonant + -y: change y to -iest
happy → the happiest

🔹 Short adjectives ending in one vowel + one consonant: double the consonant
big → the biggest
slides.tip: These are the same spelling rules as comparatives, but with -est instead of -er!

slides.title: Superlative Spelling Practice
slides.problem:
Write the superlative with the correct spelling:

1. happy → _____
2. big → _____
3. nice → _____
4. strong → _____
steps: 1. happy ends in consonant + y --> change y to i + est = the happiest
2. big ends in vowel + consonant --> double the g = the biggest
3. nice ends in -e --> just add -st = the nicest
4. strong is regular --> add -est = the strongest
slides.answer: 1. the happiest  2. the biggest  3. the nicest  4. the strongest

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🐐 Vocabulary: goat, mountain, stubborn, angry, wet
🏆 Superlatives compare ONE thing to ALL the rest
📏 Short adjectives: the + adjective + -est (the tallest, the fastest)
🌟 Long adjectives: the most + adjective (the most popular, the most exciting)
📊 Comparative = two things (-er). Superlative = all things (the -est)
